title: carex caryophyllea var. mollis
intro: Carex caryophyllea var. mollis, also known as Soft-leaved Sedge or Soft-leaved Wood Sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the Soft-leaved Sedge is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
